MY CAR HAD ONLY 600 MILES AND IS BRAND NEW. AFTER SITTING ON THE DRIVE OVERNIGHT THE CAR WOULDN'T START. THERE WAS A LOT OF CLICKING NOISES, BUT THE ENGINE WOULDN'T TURN. THAT WAS THREE WEEKS AGO. I BROUGHT IT TO THE DEALERSHIP AND THEY SAID THAT THERE WERE ERROR CODES ON THE BAT

narrative.headline: Engine, drivetrain, and electrical issues lead 2020 Malibu owner complaints
narrative.summary: Across 188 owner reports to NHTSA, engine problems are the most common, named in 31% of complaints, followed closely by power train and electrical system, each at 29%. Service brakes account for 24% and vehicle speed control for 10%. No fires were reported, but a few complaints involved crashes, and the service brakes reports include 3 injuries, so the details below are worth a read.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 58 reports (31% of all complaints). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.
